mission.title: Install and Configure Vitess on Kubernetes
mission.description: Vitess is a cloud-native horizontally-scalable distributed database system built around MySQL. It can achieve unlimited scaling through generalized sharding while keeping application code and queries agnostic to data distribution. The Vitess Operator simplifies deployment on Kubernetes.

steps.title: Install the Vitess Operator CRDs
steps.description:
Apply the Vitess Operator manifests which include the CRDs and operator deployment.
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/planetscale/vitess-operator/main/deploy/operator.yaml
```

steps.title: Verify the operator is running
steps.description:
Check that the Vitess Operator pods are running.
```bash
kubectl get pods -l app=vitess-operator
```

steps.title: Deploy a VitessCluster
steps.description:
Create a VitessCluster resource to deploy a Vitess cluster. This example deploys a minimal single-shard setup.
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: planetscale.com/v2
kind: VitessCluster
metadata:
  name: example
spec:
  images:
    vtgate: vitess/lite:v23.0.3
    vttablet: vitess/lite:v23.0.3
    vtbackup: vitess/lite:v23.0.3
    vtctld: vitess/lite:v23.0.3
    mysqld:
      mysql80Compatible: vitess/lite:v23.0.3
  cells:
  - name: zone1
    gateway:
      replicas: 1
      resources:
        requests:
          cpu: 100m
          memory: 256Mi
  keyspaces:
  - name: commerce
    turndownPolicy: Immediate
    partitionings:
    - equal:
        parts: 1
        shardTemplate:
          databaseInitScriptSecret:
            name: example-cluster-config
            key: init_db.sql
          tabletPools:
          - cell: zone1
            type: replica
            replicas: 2
            mysqld:
              resources:
                requests:
                  cpu: 100m
                  memory: 256Mi
            dataVolumeClaimTemplate:
              accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
              resources:
                requests:
                  storage: 10Gi
EOF
```

steps.title: Verify the Vitess cluster
steps.description:
Check that all Vitess components are running.
```bash
kubectl get vitessclusters
kubectl get pods -l planetscale.com/cluster=example
```

steps.title: Connect to the Vitess cluster
steps.description:
Port-forward the VTGate service to connect with a MySQL client.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/example-vtgate-zone1 3306:3306 &
mysql -h 127.0.0.1 -P 3306 -u user
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the Vitess Operator running and managing VitessCluster resources. VTGate provides MySQL-compatible access to the distributed database, while VTTablet manages the underlying MySQL instances.

uninstall.title: Delete the VitessCluster
uninstall.description: Delete the VitessCluster resource to remove all Vitess components.

uninstall.title: Remove the operator
uninstall.description: Delete the Vitess Operator deployment and CRDs.

uninstall.title: Clean up persistent volumes
uninstall.description: Delete any PVCs created by the Vitess tablets.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the operator
upgrade.description: Apply the latest operator manifests from the vitess-operator repository.

upgrade.title: Update Vitess images
upgrade.description: Update the VitessCluster resource with new image versions and apply.

troubleshooting.title: Tablets stuck in NotServing state
troubleshooting.description:
Check tablet logs and ensure the init script ran successfully.
```bash
kubectl logs -l planetscale.com/component=vttablet
```

troubleshooting.title: VTGate returns connection errors
troubleshooting.description:
Verify that tablets are healthy and the topology is correct.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/example-vtctld 15000:15000
# Access the Vitess dashboard at http://localhost:15000
```

troubleshooting.title: PVC binding issues
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure a StorageClass is configured and PVs can be dynamically provisioned.
```bash
kubectl get sc
kubectl get pvc
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher with dynamic PV provisioning and sufficient resources for running MySQL instances.
